documents.title: ARCHITECTURE MAP
documents.description: Entry point and navigation. Maps the three layers and their contracts.
documents.body:
# System Overview

This documentation describes a governed, deterministic LLM pipeline built from three layers:

- **Governance**: authority, freeze rules, version lifecycle.
- **Pipeline**: joints, gates, and routing behavior.
- **Artifacts**: schemas and templates that make execution auditable.

## Navigation

GOVERNANCE → rules that govern execution
PIPELINE → actors and checkpoints that execute and validate
ARTIFACTS → schemas/templates/examples that make work reproducible

documents.title: WHO CONTROLS YOUR AGENTS
documents.description: Every agent action traces back to a human decision. Nothing executes without a documented chain of authority.
documents.body:
# Authority Model

## Chain

1) **HUMAN (sovereign)**
2) **FREEZE RULES (default law)**
3) **AGENTS (executors)**

## Override Contract

Overrides are permitted only if:
- The target is identified.
- The justification is explicit.
- The override is logged.
- The resulting state is re-validated by gates.

## What This Prevents

- Silent policy drift.
- Undocumented escalation.
- "Helpful" rewriting that breaks invariants.

documents.title: HOW CHANGES ARE GOVERNED
documents.description: Every change is registered. Every version is auditable. Nothing is silently overwritten.
documents.body:
# Version Lifecycle

## Two Modes

### Incremental
v4.0 → v4.1 → v4.1.1 → v4.1.2 → v4.2 → v4.3
Patches and exceptions accumulate. Edge cases are documented.

### Consolidated
v5.0 absorbs prior deltas into a self-explaining baseline:
- Reduced exception load
- Unified semantics
- Fewer conditional branches

## Rule
Consolidation is not cosmetic. Consolidation is a governance event.

documents.title: HOW THE SYSTEM STAYS STABLE
documents.description: Rules have exactly four states. There is no undefined behavior. Stability is enforced, not assumed.
documents.body:
# Freeze Semantics

## State Progression

- **FROZEN**: default state. Execution suspended.
- **UNFROZEN**: override permitted + audit required.
- **ABSORBED**: exception registered into version N.
- **SIMPLIFIED**: exception becomes new law (no special-case branch).

## UI Mapping

Frozen state is visible:
- Particle flow halts.
- Track dims to 10% opacity.
- A single static dot remains centered.

The system does not disappear. It enters stillness.

documents.title: HOW EXECUTION FLOWS
documents.description: Intent enters. Governed output exits. Every step between them is validated and traceable.
documents.body:
# Pipeline Overview

## Pre-Pipeline
Inputs:
- session_state
- context

Pre-step:
- skeleton population
- session state normalization

## Main Pipeline (actors)
S1 Communicator → S2 Mediator → S3 Drafter → S4 Refiner → S5 Origin

## Outputs
- updated_session_state
- delta_brief (human-readable)
- committed artifact(s)

## Principle
A joint does work. A gate validates work. They are never the same object.

documents.title: HOW HANDOFFS ARE ENFORCED
documents.description: No agent receives input that hasn't been validated. No output leaves without verification.
documents.body:
# Joint Specifications

## S1 — Communicator
Intent → Plan
Produces: execution_plan, job_ticket

## S2 — Mediator
Plan → Constraint Packet
Produces: constraints_packet

## S3 — Drafter
Constraints → Draft Artifact
Produces: drafter_output

## S4 — Refiner
Draft → Patchlist + Verdict
Produces: patchlist (verdict: pass|fail)

## S5 — Origin
Commit on pass
Produces: origin_commit, updated session_state, delta_brief

## Retry Policy
On fail: route back to Drafter. Retry ≤ 2. Escalate after bound reached.

documents.title: WHERE EXECUTION CAN BE STOPPED
documents.description: Five checkpoints. Each one can halt execution before a bad decision propagates downstream.
documents.body:
# Gate Specifications

| Gate  | Location         | Validates              | On Fail                  |
|-------|------------------|------------------------|---------------------------|
| G_IDEM | Pipeline entry  | composite_hash match   | return cached output      |
| G1    | post-Communicator| execution_plan schema  | retry (max 2)             |
| G1A   | post-Adapter     | XML validity           | escalate                  |
| G2    | post-Mediator    | constraints_packet     | retry (max 2)             |
| G4    | post-Refiner     | verdict validity       | route by verdict          |

## UI Rule
Gate diamonds activate before their downstream joint. The checkpoint is the last visible boundary before execution passes through.

documents.title: HOW HUMANS STAY IN CONTROL
documents.description: The human defines the state. The agents execute within it. Control never transfers to the system.
documents.body:
# Orchestration Manual

## Human-as-Message-Bus
The human authors session_state and selects routing. Agents do not self-author authority.

## Procedure (high level)
1) Human provides session_state + context
2) System enters at G_IDEM
3) Execute joints with gates enforcing schema
4) Origin commits on pass
5) Human receives delta_brief

## Non-negotiable
Session state is the contract boundary. Any mutation is logged and validated.

documents.title: ARTIFACT CATALOG
documents.description: Producers, consumers, and formats for every artifact.
documents.body:
# Artifact Catalog

| Artifact          | Producer     | Consumer    | Format         |
|-------------------|--------------|-------------|----------------|
| session_state     | Origin       | all joints  | XML            |
| execution_plan    | Communicator | Mediator    | XML            |
| job_ticket        | Communicator | Mediator+   | XML (canonical)|
| constraints_packet| Mediator     | Drafter     | XML            |
| drafter_output    | Drafter      | Refiner     | XML            |
| patchlist         | Refiner      | Origin      | XML            |
| origin_commit     | Origin       | Orchestrator| XML            |
| delta_brief       | Origin       | Human       | human-readable |

## Human Exception
delta_brief is the only artifact addressed to a human.

documents.title: PROTOCOL CATALOG
documents.description: Index of protocols and their versions.
documents.body:
# Protocol Catalog

- Skeleton Population Protocol — v1.0
- Session State Generator — v1.0
- Universal Project Brief — v1.4
- Prompt Templates — v2.1

Protocol documents are self-contained: PRIME DIRECTIVE → SOURCE HIERARCHY → EXTRACTION RULES → PROCEDURE → OUTPUT SCHEMA → SPECIAL CASES → ANTI-PATTERNS.

documents.title: TEMPLATE INDEX
documents.description: Templates, joints, triggers. Deterministic routing entry points.
documents.body:
# Template Index

| Template                        | Joint        | Trigger             |
|---------------------------------|--------------|---------------------|
| T_comm_execution_plan           | Communicator | user intent         |
| T_comm_error_escalation         | Communicator | escalate verdict    |
| T_mediator_constraints_packet   | Mediator     | job_ticket          |
| T_drafter_constrained_output    | Drafter      | constraints_packet  |
| T_refiner_patchlist             | Refiner      | drafter_output      |
| T_origin_commit                 | Origin       | verdict:pass        |

Templates are contracts. They encode permitted structure and disallow improvisation.

documents.title: WHAT THE SYSTEM PRODUCES
documents.description: Every artifact has a schema. Every schema is enforced before the next stage runs.
documents.body:
# Schema Definitions

## Inventory

- session_state.xsd
- execution_plan.xsd
- constraints_packet.xsd
- drafter_output.xsd
- patchlist.xsd
- origin_commit.xsd

## Principle
If it cannot be validated, it cannot be executed.

documents.title: WHAT CHANGED AND WHY
documents.description: After every execution, a human-readable record: what changed, why it changed, what remains frozen.
documents.body:
# delta_brief (Human Output)

## Purpose
Summarize the committed delta without ambiguity.

## Required fields
- What changed (scope)
- Why it changed (justification)
- What remains frozen (boundaries)
- Verification required (if any)

## Tone
No celebration. No apology. Clear finality.
